faqs.question: What can I expect during my space flight?
faqs.answer: Your space flight is a carefully orchestrated experience: Launch begins with a thrilling 8-12 minute ascent, followed by the magical moment of reaching space. On orbital missions, you'll watch Earth shrink below as we climb to altitude. Once in space, you can unstrap and float freely, enjoy meals, conduct experiments, take photos, or simply stare at Earth through the windows. The experience is tailored to your preferences and mission type.

faqs.question: What is weightlessness like?
faqs.answer: Weightlessness is often described as the most amazing sensation humans can experience. You'll feel completely free, able to push off walls and float gracefully. Basic tasks become delightful challenges—drinking water becomes catching floating spheres, eating becomes an adventure. Most travelers adapt within hours and spend their time perfecting zero-G acrobatics. It's pure joy and freedom unlike anything on Earth.

faqs.question: What food is served on board?
faqs.answer: SpaceWing partners with award-winning chefs to create gourmet space cuisine. Meals include: steak and lobster (prepared for zero-G enjoyment), fresh fruits and vegetables, artisanal bread, fine chocolates, and premium beverages. Our space-safe champagne lets you toast in style. Special dietary requirements are accommodated, and we offer optional premium wine pairings selected for space enjoyment.

faqs.question: Can I take photos and videos?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Photography is a highlight of space travel. Personal cameras and phones are welcome (after safety verification). Large windows provide stunning photo opportunities of Earth and space. We also offer professional photography packages where our trained crew captures your experience. Many travelers broadcast their journey live to friends and family using our high-speed space internet.

faqs.question: Is there internet connectivity in space?
faqs.answer: Yes! SpaceWing provides complimentary high-speed internet via our satellite network. You can make video calls, post to social media, and livestream your experience. Connection speeds support HD video streaming. WiFi is available throughout the spacecraft, and each passenger receives a personal device with unlimited data during their mission.

faqs.question: What activities are available during the flight?
faqs.answer: Activities vary by mission but may include: zero-G floating and acrobatics, Earth observation sessions, participating in simple science experiments, virtual reality entertainment, educational presentations, spacewalk observation (for missions with EVA), meal experiences in zero-G, meditation and yoga in space, gaming tournaments, and simply relaxing while gazing at Earth.

faqs.question: How do sleeping arrangements work?
faqs.answer: On orbital and lunar missions, sleeping in zero-G is a unique pleasure. You'll strap yourself into a sleeping bag attached to the wall—no pillow needed as there's no 'up' or 'down'! Many travelers report the best sleep of their lives in space. Private sleeping quarters are available on OrbitalOne and MoonGlider, and crew quarters are available on extended missions.

faqs.question: What are the bathroom facilities like?
faqs.answer: Space toilets use gentle air suction rather than gravity. They're clean, private, and easier to use than you might expect. Training covers proper use, and our crew is available for assistance. OrbitalOne and MoonGlider feature upgraded facilities including a zero-G shower (a warm water mist experience that's surprisingly refreshing).

faqs.question: Can I communicate with people on Earth?
faqs.answer: Yes! Video calls to family and friends are a popular activity. We provide complimentary communication time, with additional time available for purchase. Many travelers schedule calls to share special moments like watching Earth rise, floating in the cupola, or toasting at a predetermined time. School groups can arrange educational Q&A sessions.

faqs.question: What happens if I feel unwell during the flight?
faqs.answer: Our crew is trained to handle any medical situation. Common issues like space motion sickness are easily managed with medication and rest. Private quarters are available if you need quiet time. For serious concerns, our medical team on the ground is available 24/7 via video consultation, and we have emergency return protocols if needed. Most travelers complete their entire mission without any issues.
